Method provenance block
Property: HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans-type, difference of orbital eigenvalues) = 6.6310857 eV
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier "standard"), density-fitted (PySCF 2.x backend)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied benzene.xyz (12 atoms) taken as-is; single-point energy/orbitals — no further relaxation (DFT//provided-geometry)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, singlet (closed shell RKS)
Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill → PySCF engine; SCF converged in 8 cycles
Caveats & convergence: SCF converged (scf_converged: true); no warnings reported by engine. Density fitting (RI) approximation active by default.

Headline result
HOMO = −7.0468329 eV
LUMO = −0.41574728 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 eV (value reported exactly as returned; at least 5 significant figures preserved)

Live log & files
The run streamed live to a frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory (path echoed at launch); JSON results saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp6_hu52l4/live.json — re-runnable via the CLI invocation embedded in that JSON (see below).

Reproduction command (from result JSON)
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp6_hu52l4/live.json
